mappings.name: Spearphishing Attachment
mappings.evidence: The initial access vector was a phishing email with a malicious archive attachment.

mappings.name: PowerShell
mappings.evidence: The archive contained a script that launched PowerShell with encoded commands.

mappings.name: LSASS Memory
mappings.evidence: The operator dumped credentials from LSASS memory.

mappings.name: SMB/Windows Admin Shares
mappings.evidence: The operator reused valid domain credentials to connect to a file server over SMB.

mappings.name: Archive via Utility
mappings.evidence: The operator compressed selected documents into a password-protected archive.

review_notes: Discovery commands may map to several techniques; review the exact command lines before accepting all discovery mappings.
Exfiltration over HTTPS may require more context before selecting a specific exfiltration technique.
